name: jobCosting
invariants: output.ok === false || (Array.isArray(output.result.items) ? typeof output.result.grandTotal === 'number' : typeof output.result.message === 'string')
output.ok === false || !Array.isArray(output.result.items) || output.result.items.every(function(i){ return Number.isFinite(i.laborCost) && Number.isFinite(i.totalCost); })
_note: Pure compute, reads artifact.data (MasonStuff channel). Per item: laborCost = round(hours × rate) [rate default 55], totalCost = laborCost + materialCost. Rollup: overhead = round((labor+materials) × 0.15), profit = round((labor+materials+overhead) × 0.10), grandTotal = labor+materials+overhead+profit. Empty/non-array items → { message }. Non-object entries are filtered; non-finite hours/rate/material floor to 0 (rate keeps its 55 default) — never NaN/Infinity. Result card renders items[].{item,laborHours,laborRate,laborCost,materialCost,totalCost}/subtotalLabor/subtotalMaterials/overhead/profit/grandTotal EXACTLY.
